L1.area: File upload processing and request parameter handling interceptors

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the file upload handling logic where an attacker can manipulate file upload parameter names to bypass existing parameter sanitization checks. This allows overriding file name and content type parameters, enabling path traversal and potentially uploading malicious files to arbitrary locations for remote code execution.

L3.area: File upload interceptor and its request parameter sanitization logic for uploaded file metadata
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the file upload interceptor where an attacker can prepend expression language prefixes to file upload parameter names (such as file name and content type) to bypass a case-insensitive equality check used for parameter sanitization. This allows overriding legitimate upload parameters, enabling path traversal and potentially uploading a malicious file which can be used for remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The file upload interceptor's parameter sanitization method uses case-insensitive equality comparison to detect duplicate parameter names, which can be bypassed using OGNL expression prefixes (e.g., 'top.uploadFileName') to override file names and enable path traversal.
